rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes
Use valid component type scope for assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports
Preserve existing subcomponents and connections when adding implementation-level bindings
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity when inserting annex blocks
Preserve existing model elements during fusion
Use valid scope for each type of contract
Do not introduce new architectural dependencies through annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use valid AGREE syntax for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees that refer to component ports in implementation scopes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not introduce new architectural dependencies through annex blocks
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
